calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-13-2013 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-03-2013 Set For Trial On Oct-15-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Deem Matters Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions; Ntc Of Submission On Pltff'S Mtn To Deem Matters Admitted; Statement Of Facts
Off Calendar Jun-11-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances on this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK FSB'S Motion to Deem Matters Admitted and for Monetary Sanctions is Denied. The prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Judge Pro Tem: Olan C. Lambert; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Deem Matters Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions; Ntc Of Submission On Pltff'S Mtn To Deem Matters Admitted; Statement Of Facts
Off Calendar Jun-03-2013 Continued To Jun-11-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances on this matter. The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK FSB, a federal savings bank's Motion to Deem Matters Admitted and for Monetary Sanctions is Continued to June 11, 2013 to give Plaintiff the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Plaintiff must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than June 3, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the [moving/opposition/reply] papers. Judge Pro Tem: Ernst Halperin; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)
